original_abstract: How do the principles of church revitalisation correlate with the divine work of the Holy Spirit? This article argues that the Spirit is the primary agent of renewal, asserting that church leaders must act as secondary agents who cooperate with his sovereign work. It is the Spirit who empowers leaders to revive, renew, and breathe new life into a declining congregation. After grounding the concept of empowerment in biblical examples, the author examines the underlying theological principles that qualify a leader for this work. The discussion then shifts to methodology, suggesting that effective strategies for revitalisation must align with the Spirit’s own way of working—specifically through the preaching of the Word and the conviction of sin. The article concludes by identifying practical signs of biblical empowerment that distinguish genuine spiritual fruit from mere pragmatic success in a local church community.
humanized_abstract: Where Does Church Revitaluation Relate to the Work of the Holy Spirit? The Holy Spirit is the Renewing Agent and Church Revitaluation Leaders are the Secondary Agents. The Holy Spirit is the renewing Agent and the role of the Church Revitaluation Leader is to be a secondary agent cooperating with Him as the primary renewing Agent. The Holy Spirit is the One who gives strength to leaders to bring life to a dead church. Scripture gives us many examples of the work of the Holy Spirit in relation to empowerment. Understanding these biblical truths will provide us with the necessary theological grounding as we look at what character qualifications are necessary in a Church Revitaluation Leader. We will also look at how methodology must reflect the way that the Holy Spirit works in order to bring about a successful Church Revitaluation effort such as through preaching and convicting people of their need of salvation. We will close by identifying some biblical indicators of scriptural empowerment which serve to distinguish spiritual fruit from merely fleshly results in the life of a local church.
